page_content:
TLPL770 Black and Latino Education: History and Policy (3 Credits) Examination of the historical legacies and contemporary policies that have contributed to the educational status (K-20) of Blacks and Latinos in the United States from the 19th century to the present. Issues surrounding language, immigration, racism, social class, and state and federal policies will also be analyzed. Recommended: EDCI776; and completion of EDSP611 recommended. Credit Only Granted for: EDCI786 or TLPL770. Formerly: EDCI786.

TLPL777 Critical Race Theory in Education (3 Credits) This course asks: what is the function of racism, and how do we work toward a more racially just education system, if racism is permanent? Students learn tenets of Critical Race Theory and related frameworks (e.g., BlackCrit, AsianCrit, UndocuCrit, LatCrit, TribalCrit) and engage in counterstorytelling to amplify the experiences of Black, Indigenous, Latine, Asian, and other non-Black people of Color. Recommended: TLPL776. Credit Only Granted for: TLPL777 or TLPL788G. Formerly: TLPL788G.
